start.speaker: old_maren
start.text: I felt you coming. The roots told me — your footsteps are heavier today. Something weighs on you.

options.text: Tell me a story, Old Maren. About the forest.
Is there anything I can bring you from the forest?
The priest who shares your name — what do you really think of him?
I should go. Thank you, Old Maren.

parable_sea_king.speaker: old_maren
parable_sea_king.text: There was once a king who lived by the sea. The sea gave him fish, calm waters, and safe passage for his ships. In return, the king promised one pearl from every catch.

parable_2.speaker: old_maren
parable_2.text: One year, the king grew greedy. He kept the pearls. The sea said nothing. Two years. Three. On the fourth year, the sea took his harbor. On the fifth, his ships. On the sixth... his son.

parable_3.speaker: old_maren
parable_3.text: The king offered ten thousand pearls to get his son back. The sea refused. 'I did not ask for pearls,' it said. 'I asked for one. Freely given. You chose to make it a debt. Debts are paid differently.'

parable_lesson.speaker: old_maren
parable_lesson.text: The forest is patient, child. But patience has limits. And when the patient lose patience... nothing can stand against the tide.

moonpetal_quest.speaker: old_maren
moonpetal_quest.text: There is a flower that grows at the forest's edge — the moonpetal. It only blooms at night, and only where the roots run deepest. If you bring me one, I can brew a tea that will let you hear the forest's voice. Briefly.

moonpetal_2.speaker: old_maren
moonpetal_2.text: But be warned — the forest knows when you're taking from it. Go gently. Ask permission. And whatever you do... do not step off the path the talisman shows you.

priest_opinion.speaker: old_maren
priest_opinion.text: That man is no more a priest than I am a songbird. He carries the Church's banner, but his hands smell of nightshade and his prayers smell of ambition. The Ashen Light burned our groves — and now they send this fox to gnaw at the roots we still tend.

priest_opinion_2.speaker: old_maren
priest_opinion_2.text: But I am biased — I admit it freely. The old wounds between druids and the Church run deep. Perhaps he is sincere. Perhaps. But I have lived long enough to know the difference between piety and performance.
